name: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Subpart D – Walking-Working Surfaces
source_authority: U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)

rules.label: General Walking-Working Surfaces
rules.notes_template: Verify walking-working surfaces are kept in good repair, free of hazards, with adequate drainage; design loads must be sufficient for maximum intended load.
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.22 – Walking-working surfaces shall be kept clean, dry, and free of hazards. Surfaces shall be designed and constructed for maximum intended load.
rules.remediation: Document maintenance program and structural design loads.

rules.label: Fixed Ladders
rules.notes_template: Verify fixed ladders > 24 ft above lower level: ladder safety system or personal fall arrest system required (cages no longer satisfy on new installations after Nov 2018; full conversion required by Nov 2036).
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.23(d) – Fixed ladders extending more than 24 ft above a lower level shall be equipped with a personal fall arrest system or ladder safety system.
rules.remediation: Specify ladder safety system (rigid rail or cable) or PFAS on fixed ladders > 24 ft.

rules.label: Stairways – General Industry
rules.notes_template: Verify general-industry stairs: width ≥ 22 in., riser height ≤ 9.5 in., tread depth ≥ 9.5 in., uniform riser/tread, slope between 30° and 50°, handrails required.
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.25 – Stairway design parameters for general industry occupancies.
rules.remediation: Detail stairs per §1910.25; provide handrails on stair widths and configurations as required.

rules.label: Fall Protection – 4-Foot Trigger
rules.notes_template: Verify fall protection at unprotected sides/edges ≥ 4 ft above lower level (general industry): guardrail (42 in. ± 3), safety net, or PFAS. Roof work has specific provisions in §1910.28(b)(13).
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.28 – Employees on walking-working surfaces 4 ft or more above lower levels shall be protected by guardrail, safety net, or PFAS.
rules.remediation: Provide compliant guardrail at every edge ≥ 4 ft, or specify PFAS anchorages and rescue plan.

rules.label: Guardrails / Handrails / Stair Rails
rules.notes_template: Verify guardrail top-rail 42 in. ± 3 above walking surface, mid-rail at midpoint, withstand 200 lbf top-rail / 150 lbf mid-rail, with 4-in. toe board where required.
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.29 – Design specifications for guardrails, handrails, and stair rails.
rules.remediation: Detail guardrails to §1910.29 strength and dimensional specs; coordinate with toe boards.
